top_findings.summary: The skill requests the ability to run any Bash command (Bash(cmd:*)), which is excessive for its stated primary purpose of managing version bumper operations. While version bumping may require specific commands like `git` or file editing tools, allowing unrestricted Bash access could enable unrelated or potentially harmful system actions beyond the scope of version control tasks, such as accessing sensitive directories or executing arbitrary code. A more restricted set of commands tailored to version management would be sufficient and safer.
